briefdescription.para: < Prototype declarations for PL eFUSE interface functions

detaileddescription.para: Programs a bit of PL eFUSE row.

parameterdescription.para: - row number
- bit position

para.verbatim: - XST_FAILURE - In case of failure - XST_SUCCESS - In case of Success

simplesect.para: Updates the global variable ErrorCode with error code(if any).

inbodydescription.para: Check if the row position is valid.
Check if the bit position is valid.
If row=0 then bit should be either 1 to 5 and 8 to 10, 15 to 19 and 22 to 24 rest all are not supported
Monitor the Voltage and temperature using XADC, if out of range return unique error.

briefdescription.para: Programs PL eFUSE row.

parameterdescription.para: - row number
- row data pointer

para.verbatim: - XST_FAILURE - In case of failure - XST_SUCCESS - In case of Success

simplesect.para: Updates the global variable ErrorCode with error code(if any).

inbodydescription.para: check if row_data is not NULL

briefdescription.para: Programs PL eFUSE Control Register.

parameterdescription.para: - Control data pointer

para.verbatim: - XST_FAILURE - In case of failure - XST_SUCCESS - In case of Success

simplesect.para: Updates the global variable ErrorCode with error code(if any).

inbodydescription.para: check if cntrl_data is not NULL
Read the FUSE_CNTRL register
check if FUSE_CNTRL allows us to write FUSE_CNTRL eFUSE array for Zynq.
This means we cannot program FUSE_CNTRL register

memberdef.argsstring: (u8 Row, u8 Bit, u8 MarginOption, u8 *BitData)
memberdef.name: XilSKey_EfusePl_ReadBit

briefdescription.para: Reads a PL eFUSE bit & stores.

parameterdescription.para: - row number
- bit position in the specified row
- Margin Option(One of the reading method of PLeFUSE)
- Place holder to store the read value

para.verbatim: - XST_FAILURE - In case of failure - XST_SUCCESS - In case of Success

simplesect.para: Updates the global variable ErrorCode with error code(if any).

inbodydescription.para: check if row_data is not NULL
Check if the bit position is valid.
If row=0 then bit should be either 1 to 5 and 8 to 10, 15 to 19 and 22 to 24 rest all are not supported

briefdescription.para: Reads row data of a specified row.

parameterdescription.para: - row number
- Margin Option(One of the reading method of PLeFUSE)
- To store the read data bytes of specified row

para.verbatim: - XST_FAILURE - In case of failure - XST_SUCCESS - In case of Success

simplesect.para: Read data will be stored

inbodydescription.para: Check if the row position is valid.
Check the read margin option.
check if row_data is not NULL
Monitor the Voltage and temperature using XADC, if out of range return unique error.
Here we have to use the impact algorithm to read the eFUSE row. and return the data in row_data.

briefdescription.para: Reads PL eFUSE Control Register data.

parameterdescription.para: - Place holder to store the read data (control register)

para.verbatim: - XST_FAILURE - In case of failure - XST_SUCCESS - In case of Success

simplesect.para: Updates the global variable ErrorCode with error code (if any)

inbodydescription.para: check if cntrl_data is not NULL

briefdescription.para: Verify the PL eFUSE bit blown by reading it back in all possible margin reads (Normal, Margin 1 and Margin 2).

parameterdescription.para: - row number
- bit position of the specified row

para.verbatim: - XST_FAILURE - In case of failure - XST_SUCCESS - In case of Success

simplesect.para: Updates the global variable ErrorCode with error code(if any).

briefdescription.para: Checks whether data bits (0-29) of an PL eFUSE row are all zeroes or not.

parameterdescription.para: - row data pointer

para.verbatim: - XST_FAILURE - In case of failure - XST_SUCCESS - In case of Success

simplesect.para: None.

briefdescription.para: Calculates and stores the ECC data of row data.

parameterdescription.para: - Pointer to row data on which ECC will be calculated
- Pointer to store the calculated ECC

simplesect.para: None.

inbodydescription.para: This is the DED data

briefdescription.para: Verify's the AES key by passing the CRC of expected key.

parameterdescription.para: is CRC of expected AES key

simplesect.para: None.

briefdescription.para: Programs PL eFUSE of Zynq.

parameterdescription.para: is an instance of EFuse PL of Zynq.

simplesect.para: Updates the global variable ErrorCode with error code(if any).

inbodydescription.para: Read the FUSE_CNTL register bits [5:2], and if any of them is found to be set to 1 then we can not write to the eFUSE, so return with unique error.
If everything is well above then start programming with FUSE_AES key as passed to this function, followed by FUSE_USER if selected.

AES key is 256 bits, but has to be written across many rows, in the PL eFUSE each row has 24 bits of data bits and 6 bits of ecc bits. So for 256 bits we will need full 10 rows(240 bits) and extra 1 row for remaining 16 bits, but rest 8 bits will be taken from the FUSE_USER key value[7:0]
check if FUSE_CNTRL allows us to read and write the AES and USER eFUSE array.
convert each aes data bits to bytes.
convert each user data bits to bytes.
check if row 20 to 30 are empty before programming AES and USER low key.
program AES_KEY 256 bits and USER_KEY lower 8 bits first.
prepare row data for row from 20 to 29.
prepare row data for row 30.
check if 31 is empty before programming USER high key.
Program USER_KEY high 24 bits next. Prepare row data for row 31.

briefdescription.para: Programs a AES key rows for PL eFUSE of Ultrascale.

simplesect.para: Updates the global variable ErrorCode with error code(if any).

inbodydescription.para: check if row 20 to 27 are empty before programming AES Key
Verify AES key is zero or not before programming AES key
program AES_KEY 256 bits

memberdef.argsstring: (u8 RowStart, u8 RowEnd, u8 *DataPrgrmg, u8 Page)
memberdef.name: XilSKey_EfusePl_Program_RowRange_ultra

briefdescription.para: Programs a range of rows for PL eFUSE of Ultrascale.

parameterdescription.para: is the starting row to be programmed.
is the row till which need to be programmed.
is the data to be programmed.
is the Page to be programmed.

simplesect.para: Updates the global variable ErrorCode with error code(if any).

inbodydescription.para: check if DataPrgrmg is not NULL
Check is RowStart to RowEnd of given Page are empty before programming.This check is only for RSA hash programming
Program rows

briefdescription.para: This API gets the row data of Ored Normal and Redundant rows for EFUSE of Ultrascale.

parameterdescription.para: is the row number.
is the ORed result of both rows.
indicates row belongs to which page.

simplesect.para: Updates the global variable ErrorCode with error code(if any).

inbodydescription.para: check if RowData is not NULL

memberdef.argsstring: (u8 RowStart, u8 RowEnd, u8 *KeyRead, u8 Page)
memberdef.name: XilSKey_EfusePl_GetDataRowRange_Ultra

briefdescription.para: This API gets the rows data of given range of FUSE's array of Ultrascale.

parameterdescription.para: is the starting row to be programmed.
is the row till which need to be programmed.
is a pointer of array in which read data is updated.
is the Page to be programmed.

simplesect.para: Updates the global variable ErrorCode with error code(if any). Reads all the margin options.

briefdescription.para: Reads the PL efuse key (AES and user) of Zynq.

parameterdescription.para: - Input data to be written to PL eFUSE

simplesect.para: Updates the global variable ErrorCode with error code(if any).

briefdescription.para: Reads control register and checks the conditions for reading keys.

parameterdescription.para: is an instance of PL efuse of ultrascale

simplesect.para: None.

briefdescription.para: This function checks the conditions for keys programming.

parameterdescription.para: is an instance of EFusePL of Ultrascale.

simplesect.para: None.

memberdef.argsstring: (u8 Row, u8 Bit, u8 Redundant, u8 Page)
memberdef.name: XilSKey_EfusePl_ProgramBit_Ultra

briefdescription.para: Programs the bit of given normal or redundant row of PL efuse key for Ultrascale.

parameterdescription.para: is the row number of Fuse array.
is the bit position to be read.
is the option to be selected either redundant row or normal row.
is the page of Fuse array in which row has to be read

simplesect.para: Updates the global variable ErrorCode with error code(if any).

memberdef.argsstring: (u8 Row, u8 *RowData, u8 Redundant, u8 Page)
memberdef.name: XilSKey_EfusePl_ProgramRow_Ultra

briefdescription.para: Programs the entire row PL efuse key for Ultrascale.

parameterdescription.para: is the row number of Fuse array.
is a pointer to a variable to program the row.
is the option to be selected either redundant row or normal row.
is the page of Fuse array in which row has to be read

simplesect.para: Updates the global variable ErrorCode with error code(if any).

inbodydescription.para: check if row_data is not NULL

briefdescription.para: Programs PL eFUSE reads Secure Register of Ultrascale.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to which holds the data read from Secure register of Fuse.

inbodydescription.para: check if Pointer passed is not NULL
check if FUSE_CNTRL allows us to write FUSE_SEC eFUSE array for Ultrascale series.
This means we cannot program FUSE_CNTRL register

briefdescription.para: Programs PL eFUSE Secure Register of Ultrascale.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to which holds the data to be programmed.

check if FUSE_CNTRL allows us to write FUSE_SEC eFUSE array for Ultrascale series.
This means we cannot program FUSE_CNTRL register

memberdef.argsstring: (u8 Row, u8 Bit, u8 MarginOption, u8 *BitData, u8 Redundant, u8 Page)
memberdef.name: XilSKey_EfusePl_ReadBit_Ultra

briefdescription.para: Reads the bit of given normal or redundant row of PL efuse key for Ultrascale.

parameterdescription.para: is the row number of Fuse array.
is the bit position to be read.
is the option to select in which mode read has to be happened
is a pointer to a variable to store the read value of given given bit in provided row.
is the option to be selected either redundant row or normal row.
is the page of Fuse array in which row has to be read

simplesect.para: Updates the global variable ErrorCode with error code(if any). Using this API can't read AES key bits of rows(20-27) as they can't read directly.

inbodydescription.para: check if row_data is not NULL
Check if the row position is valid.

memberdef.argsstring: (u32 Row, u8 MarginOption, u8 *RowDataBytes, u8 Redundant, u8 Page)
memberdef.name: XilSKey_EfusePl_ReadRow_Ultra

briefdescription.para: Reads the given normal or redundant row of PL efuse key for Ultrascale.

parameterdescription.para: is the row number of Fuse array.
is the option to select in which mode read has to be happened
is a pointer to an array to store the read value of given row.
is the option to be selected either redundant row or normal row.
is the page of Fuse array in which row has to be read

simplesect.para: Updates the global variable ErrorCode with error code(if any). Using this API can't read AES key rows(20-27) as they can't read directly.

inbodydescription.para: Check if the row position is valid.
Check if the row position is valid.
Check the read margin option.
check if row_data is not NULL
Here we have to use the impact algorithm to read the eFUSE row. and return the data in row_data.

memberdef.name: XilSkey_EfusePl_VerifyBit_Ultra

briefdescription.para: Verifies PL eFUSE bit of Ultrascale.

parameterdescription.para: specifies the row number to be verified.
specifies bit number to be verified.
is the option to be selected either redundant row or normal row.
is the page of Fuse array in which row has to be read

para.verbatim: - XST_FAILURE - In case of failure - XST_SUCCESS - In case of Success

simplesect.para: None.

briefdescription.para: This function throws an error if user requests already programmed User FUSE bit to revert, and copies the bits to be programmed in particular row into provided UserFuses_TobePrgrmd pointer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to user requested programming bits of an User FUSE row.
holds User FUSE row bits which needs to be programmed actually.
specifies the User key size.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE: Returns error if user requests programmed bit to revert
XST_SUCCESS: If User requests valid bits.

simplesect.para: If user requests a non-zero bit for making to zero throws an error which is not possible.

briefdescription.para: Programs PL eFUSE Control Register of Ultrascale.

parameterdescription.para: - Control data pointer

para.verbatim: - XST_FAILURE - In case of failure - XST_SUCCESS - In case of Success

simplesect.para: Updates the global variable ErrorCode with error code(if any).

inbodydescription.para: check if FUSE_CNTRL allows us to write FUSE_CNTRL eFUSE array for Ultrascale series.
This means we cannot program FUSE_CNTRL register

memberdef.argsstring: (u8 Row, u8 Bit, u8 Redundant, u8 Page)
memberdef.name: XilSKey_EfusePl_Ultra_Check

briefdescription.para: This API checks whether the parameters passed for programming or reading is correct.

parameterdescription.para: is the row number of Fuse array.
is the bit position to be read.
is the option to be selected either redundant row or normal row.
is the page of Fuse array in which row has to be read

simplesect.para: Updates the global variable ErrorCode with error code(if any).

inbodydescription.para: Check if the row position is valid.
Check if the bit position is valid.
If row = 1 then bit should be either 0 to 2 and 5 to 9 and 15 rest all are not supported
If row = 10 then bits should be supported from 0 to 5
Check if the row position is valid.
Check if the bit position is valid.
If row = 2 or 3 then bit should be either 0 to 2 and 5 to 9 and 15 rest all are not supported
If row = 4 then bits should be supported from 0 to 5

briefdescription.para: This function gets the row data of Ored Normal and Redundant rows for EFUSE of Ultrascale and ultrascale plus.

detaileddescription.para: For ultrascale plus, read row contains both redundant and normal bits upper 16 bits contains redundant bits and lower 16 bits contain normal bits, each row will have only 16 valid bits.

parameterdescription.para: is the row number.
is the ORed result of both rows.
indicates row belongs to which page.

simplesect.para: Updates the global variable ErrorCode with error code(if any).

briefdescription.para: Fuse Ctrl Row.
AES Key size.
32 bit User key size
128 bit User key size

128 bit USER key end row of FUSE for Ultrascale plus series

memberdef.argsstring: (unsigned char row, unsigned int *row_data, unsigned char marginOption)
memberdef.name: JtagRead

briefdescription.para: JTAG Server Read routine.

memberdef.argsstring: (u8 Row, u8 Bit, u8 Page, u8 Redundant)
memberdef.name: JtagWrite_Ultrascale

briefdescription.para: This function blows the fuse of Ultrascale with provided parameters.

parameterdescription.para: specifies the row number of EFUSE to blow.
Specifies the bit location in the given row.
tell the page of EFUSE in which the given row is located.

is a flag to specify the bit to be programmed is Normal bit or Redundant bit.
Redundant - XSK_EFUSEPL_REDUNDANT_ULTRA
Normal - XSK_EFUSEPL_NORMAL_ULTRA

simplesect.para: None.
None.

memberdef.argsstring: (u8 Row, u32 *RowData, u8 MarginOption, u8 Page, u8 Redundant)
memberdef.name: JtagRead_Ultrascale

briefdescription.para: This function reads entire row of Ultrascale's EFUSE.

parameterdescription.para: specifies the row number of EFUSE.
is a variable which tells the margin option in which read operation to be performed.
tell the page of EFUSE in which the given row is located.

is a flag to specify the bit to be programmed is Normal bit or Redundant bit.
Redundant - XSK_EFUSEPL_REDUNDANT_ULTRA
Normal - XSK_EFUSEPL_NORMAL_ULTRA This variable is not valid for Ultrascale plus as each row contains both redundant and normal bits upper 16 are redundant.

simplesect.para: None.

Method to read FUSE register in Direct Macro Access way. Go to TLR to clear FUSE_CTS Load FUSE_CTS instruction on IR Step to CDR/SDR to shift in 32-bits FUSE_CTS command word Shift in MAGIC_CTS_WRITE "FEED28AC" for ultrascale Read 64 bit data in IR read state.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the status row of Ultrascale's EFUSE and updates the pointer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to a 32 bit variable which stores the status register value read from EFUSE status register.

simplesect.para: None.

Method to read FUSE register in Direct Macro Access way. For reading the status values we need send the bitstream in shift DR state. Shift in MAGIC_CTS_WRITE "FEED28AC" for ultrascale Read 64 bit data in IR read state.

briefdescription.para: This function verifies the AES key of Ultrascale's EFUSE with provided CRC value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to a 32 bit variable which holds the expected AES key's CRC.
is a variable which tells the margin option in which read operation to be performed.

simplesect.para:
Returns XST_FAILURE/XST_SUCCESS
XST_SUCCESS - If CRC is correct
XST_FAILURE - If CRC is wrong

To verify AES key with provided CRC 256 bits need to be written with all ZEROS and last 32 bits with CRC. And then CTS word should be framed and read 9 rows. The keys are stored in 8 rows (20 to 27) but in UltraScale the device will read all 1s. On the 9th read (row 28), the CRC computation takes place. An extra read after the 9th read is required to read the computed CRC out (can use last row addr of 28 or row 0). The FPGA internally compares the computed CRC with the expected CRC loaded through the FUSE_KEY operation. If they match, then the expected CRC is read. If not, the FPGA will return all 1s.

briefdescription.para: Initializes PL eFUSE with input data given.

parameterdescription.para: - Input data to be written to PL eFUSE

para.verbatim: - XST_FAILURE - In case of failure - XST_SUCCESS - In case of Success

simplesect.para: Updates the global variable ErrorCode with error code(if any).

inbodydescription.para: Start using the Jtag server to read the JTAG ID and compare with the stored ID, if it not matches return with unique error code. By reading the Jtag ID we will be sure that the JTAG related stuff is working as expected.
If everything is ok then return PASS.

briefdescription.para: Programs PL eFUSE with input data given through InstancePtr.

parameterdescription.para: - Pointer to PL eFUSE instance which holds the input data to be written to PL eFUSE.

simplesect.para: When this API is called: Initializes the timer, XADC/xsysmon and JTAG server subsystems. Returns an error in the following cases, if the reference clock frequency is not in the range or if the PL DAP ID is not identified, if the system is not in a position to write the requested PL eFUSE bits (because the bits are already written or not allowed to write) if the temperature and voltage are not within range.

inbodydescription.para: Start using the Jtag server to read the JTAG ID and compare with the stored ID, if it not matches return with unique error code. By reading the Jtag ID we will be sure that the JTAG related stuff is working as expected.
If everything is ok then return PASS.

briefdescription.para: Reads the PL efuse status bits and gets all secure and control bits.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to PL eFUSE instance.
Buffer to store the status bits read.

para.verbatim: - XST_FAILURE - In case of failure - XST_SUCCESS - In case of Success

briefdescription.para: Reads the PL efuse keys and stores them in the corresponding arrays in instance structure.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to PL eFUSE instance.

para.verbatim: - XST_FAILURE - In case of failure - XST_SUCCESS - In case of Success

simplesect.para: This function initializes the timer, XADC and JTAG server subsystems, if not already done so. In Zynq - Reads AES key and User keys. In Ultrascale - Reads 32 bit and 128 bit User keys and RSA hash But AES key cannot be read directly it can be verified with CRC check (for that we need to update the instance with 32 bit CRC value, API updates whether provided CRC value is matched with actuals or not). To calculate the CRC of expected AES key one can use any of the following APIs XilSKey_CrcCalculation() or XilSkey_CrcCalculation_AesKey()

detaileddescription.para:
Contains the function definitions for the PL eFUSE functionality.
MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes 1.00a rpoolla 04/26/13 First release 1.02a hk 10/28/13 Added API's to read status bits and key.PR# 735957 2.00 hk 22/01/14 Corrected PL voltage checks to VCCINT and VCCAUX. CR#768077 2.1 kvn 04/01/15 Fixed warnings. CR#716453. 3.00 vns 31/07/15 Added efuse functionality for Ultrascale. 4.0 vns 10/01/15 provided conditional compilation to support ZynqMp platform also. Corrected error code names of Ultrascale efuse PL 5.0 vns 07/01/16 Verification of programming bits is done by performing all Margin reads. Added conditions for programming control and secure bits. 6.00 vns 29/06/16 Added Margin 2 read verification after programming every Zynq's eFUSE PL bit CR #953052. 07/07/16 Modified XilSKey_EfusePl_ProgramBit_Ultra such that it returns error code when JtagWrite_Ultrascale fails programming eFUSE bit. Error occurs only when Hardware Module has encountered timeout. 26/07/16 Added 128 bit user key programming and reading. Provided single bit programming feature for 32 and 128 bit user keys for eFUSE Ultrascale. 6.4 vns 02/27/18 Added support for programming secure bit 6 - enable obfuscation feature for eFUSE AES key 6.6 vns 06/06/18 Added doxygen tags 6.7 psl 03/20/19 Added eFuse key write support for SSIT devices. arc 04/04/19 Fixed CPP warnings. psl 04/15/19 Added JtagServerInit function. 6.8 psl 05/21/19 Added else case to clear UserFuses_TobePrgrmd psl 08/23/19 Added Debug define to avoid writing of eFuse. vns 08/29/19 Initialized Status variables 6.9 vns 03/18/20 Fixed Armcc compilation errors 7.2 am 07/13/21 Fixed doxygen warnings 7.3 har 11/15/21 Removed local variable ErrorCode in XilSKey_EfusePl_GetRowData_Ultra() 7.8 vss 09/29/25 Fixed efuse read mask
